items.question: pathlib vs os.path — which should you use and why?
items.answer: Prefer pathlib: it offers object-oriented paths, the / join operator, and convenience methods (read_text, glob, mkdir) that are more readable and less error-prone. os.path is the older, function-based API kept for compatibility.

items.question: What breaks if you join paths with '/'.join([...]) or '+' on Windows?
items.answer: You get wrong separators and broken paths because Windows uses backslashes. Path(...) / ... produces the correct separator on every OS.

items.question: Where should API keys come from at runtime?
items.answer: From the environment via os.environ (set by the shell, container or secrets manager), not from source code, so they can vary per environment and stay out of version control.
